news.title: Telangana government yet to set up municipal building tribunal despite HC directive in 2022
news.para: Despite a decade-old provision and a four-year-old assurance to the Telangana High Court, the state government has failed to establish the Municipal Building Tribunal (MBT). This delay exacerbates the mushrooming of unauthorized structures, leaving thousands of cases pending in courts. The tribunal, intended to streamline building-related disputes, remains elusive, impacting effective governance and legal recourse.

news.title: Maharashtra government to set up new IT park near proposed Purandar airport
news.para: Maharashtra government announced a new IT park near the proposed international airport in Purandar. The move aims to attract IT companies by offering better infrastructure, potentially creating one lakh jobs. Experts urged the government to learn from Hinjewadi's past mistakes and ensure world-class amenities.

news.title: Kochi: After years-long wait, Bliss City project to become reality
news.para: Kochi Metro Rail Ltd's ambitious Bliss City project in Kakkanad is moving forward with the leasing of its first land parcels. This 31.43-acre mixed-use development, a collaboration with NBCC, will feature commercial, shopping, and entertainment facilities, including a proposed theme park and I-Max theatre, aiming to become a premier business and leisure destination.

news.title: Developers, RWAs in Gurugram told to submit action-taken reports on complaints on structural safety
news.para: Gurugram's district administration will convene a meeting on March 17 with developers and RWAs to address non-implementation of structural audit repair recommendations. Residents have alleged that crucial repair and strengthening measures for several group housing colonies have not been properly executed. The meeting aims to review compliance status and identify pending works to ensure building safety.

news.title: Telangana RERA imposes ₹4.7 crore penalty on Bharathi Builders for pre-lauch sales
news.para: Telangana RERA has imposed a record Rs 4.7 crore penalty on Bharathi Builders for illegal pre-launch sales of its 'Bharathi Lake View Apartments' project. The builder collected substantial amounts from buyers without RERA registration and failed to commence construction or deliver the project within the promised timeline. Aggrieved homebuyers will receive full refunds with interest.

news.title: ED names Valmark Realty Holdings' director, Bengaluru civic body employees in TDR scam
news.para: Enforcement Directorate filed a prosecution complaint in a TDR scam involving Valmark Realty Holdings director and 19 others, including BBMP employees. The probe, based on an ACB FIR, alleges illegal profit of Rs 27.7 crore through fraudulent TDR procurement. Brokers, landowners, and officials conspired to obtain TDR by misrepresenting property status and valuations.

news.title: China's Fantasia unveils sweeping $4.7 billion debt overhaul plan
news.para: Fantasia, a Chinese property developer, has detailed its offshore debt restructuring. The plan involves issuing new shares, convertible bonds, and secured notes. This aims to settle approximately $4.66 billion in offshore debt. The developer defaulted in 2021 amid a real estate crisis. This move seeks to regain financial stability and creditor confidence.

news.title: UK homebuilders warn Middle East war compounds affordability crisis
news.para: UK homebuilders face renewed pressure as the war in Iran threatens to increase building costs and maintain high interest rates. Companies like Berkeley Group, Vistry, and Persimmon are navigating a challenging market, with rising energy prices impacting material costs. While some builders report recovering sales, geopolitical events and economic uncertainty continue to weigh on the industry's profitability and demand.

news.title: Mumbai residents discuss pollution at building sites with mayor
news.para: In a powerful gathering, residents from the Western suburbs, comprising a vast community, engaged Mayor Ritu Tawde about their serious worries regarding pollution and excessive noise from nearby construction sites. They advocated for tighter regulations on work hours, enforced measures for dust suppression, and improved noise monitoring systems overseen by the BMC.

news.title: MHADA to push redevelopment of old layouts in Mumbai, Pune and Nashik
news.para: Maharashtra's real estate market is experiencing a remarkable transformation driven by the redevelopment of brownfield sites. The Maharashtra Housing and Area Development Authority (MHADA) is placing emphasis on revamping outdated housing schemes in key urban areas, notably Mumbai, Pune, and Nashik.

news.title: NBCC to execute ₹480 crore phase-I of Marine Eco City project in Kochi
news.para: NBCC (India) will serve as the project management consultant for the first phase of Kochi's Marine Eco City project, a mixed-use development on 17.9 acres. The initial phase, costing approximately ₹480 crore, will feature a residential-cum-commercial tower with 152 apartments, retail, and office spaces. Subsequent phases will include a five-star hotel, convention center, and additional residential towers.

news.title: Pune: Co-op department dissolves Lohegaon housing society body for denying member access to documents
news.para: Pune's deputy registrar of cooperative society dissolved an 18-member housing society managing committee for denying a member access to crucial financial documents. The committee members have been disqualified for five years. This action follows repeated reminders and directives from cooperative officials, upholding members' legal right to inspect society records.

news.title: Ulhasnagar: UMC launches ‘Abhay Yojana’, offers 100% waiver on property tax penalties from March 13–16
news.para: Ulhasnagar Municipal Corporation introduces a four-day 'Abhay Yojana' from March 13-16, 2026, waiving all penalty charges on pending property and water taxes. This initiative, spurred by MLA Kumar Ailani's request, offers a 100% waiver on delayed penalties for lump-sum payments, encouraging citizens to clear dues and bolstering civic finances.

news.title: India’s listed REIT market cap rises over six-fold to ₹1.73 lakh crore since FY20: Report
news.para: India's listed REIT market capitalization has surged over six-fold to ₹1.73 lakh crore by FY26, driven by new listings and rising unit prices. Regulatory changes, including SEBI's reclassification and potential inclusion in equity indices, alongside RBI's lending plans and CPSE asset monetization, are poised to further accelerate growth and expand investor participation.

news.title: Consider establishing specialised tribunal for building disputes: Telangana HC
news.para: The Telangana High Court suggested the state government establish a specialized tribunal for building disputes to alleviate judicial burden. This observation came during a hearing of an appeal by residents challenging a builder's permit, which was previously dismissed by a single judge. The court adjourned the matter to April 15.

news.title: Investors allege ₹1.6 crore fraud in WTC GIFT City scheme
news.para: CID (Crime) has registered a complaint alleging financial fraud exceeding Rs 1.56 crore in a GIFT City investment scheme. Investors were promised high monthly returns and a buyback guarantee for commercial units, but their funds were allegedly not repaid after payments stopped in September 2022. Post-dated cheques for repayment were dishonoured, prompting an investigation into the alleged fraud.

news.title: Illegal colonies, walls and sheds razed by DTCP in Gurugram
news.para: The Department of Town and Country Planning (DTCP) intensified its campaign against illegal constructions this week, conducting demolition drives in Sadhrana, Bhondsi, and Sector 89. Authorities razed unauthorized colonies and structures, including foundations, boundary walls, and sheds, to curb encroachment and protect buyers from misleading developments. The action aimed to prevent further illegal plotting and sale of land.

news.title: Magistrate’s court to hear ₹140 crore Guruashish cheating case
news.para: A special court in Mumbai transferred a Rs 140 crore cheating and conspiracy case against Guruashish Construction and its directors to a magistrate's court. The CBI found no evidence of public servant involvement, leading the special court to state it lacked jurisdiction to proceed with the matter against private individuals alone.

news.title: Centre extends PMAY-U 2.0 scheme till September 2026; targets housing for one crore urban families
news.para: The Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban is now extended until September 2026, with the launch of PMAY-U 2.0. This upgrade will benefit an estimated one crore more urban households over the next five years. The scheme is not just about building homes; it's about embracing modern building practices and sustainable living solutions.

news.title: Scheme to incubate eight new cities cannot be implemented: Housing ministry
news.para: The government has decided to abandon its plan for the development of eight new cities, explaining that inadequate planning frameworks rendered the Rs 8,000 crore grant from the 15th Finance Commission unusable. With a keen eye on effective fund management, the ministry has opted to reallocate the budget towards bolstering seven established city economic zones, aiming for more sustainable growth.
